citations.quote: Perform a comprehensive GAP assessment of your current AML/CFT/CPF Policies, Procedures, Systems & Controls
across Dubai's free zones and mainland, except within the jurisdiction of Dubai International Financial Centre

summary: On 24 November 2025, VARA (the Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority, which licenses and supervises virtual asset activity across Dubai's mainland and free zones, excluding the Dubai International Financial Centre) circulated a notice to all Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs) it licenses, announcing Federal Decree-Law No. (10) of 2025 on anti-money laundering, counter-terrorism financing and proliferation financing (AML/CFT/CPF), issued 30 September 2025 and effective from 14 October 2025. The Decree-Law brings VASPs within the AML/CFT/CPF supervisory perimeter and updates preventive measures, transparency, beneficial-ownership rules and penalties. VARA's circular makes a GAP Assessment mandatory: a completed, clause-by-clause report and board-approved remediation plan are due within 60 calendar days, priority fixes and staff training within 90 days, and remaining gaps closed with signed-off completion within 120 days, backed by mandatory recordkeeping.
title: Publication of UAE Federal Decree-Law on AML/CFT/CPF (2025) — Mandatory GAP Assessment for VASPs
why_it_matters: VARA is the regulator that licenses and supervises Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs) operating in Dubai outside the DIFC free zone. This circular is VARA's formal notice that a new federal anti-money-laundering law now binds those firms directly, with fixed 60/90/120-day compliance deadlines and the threat of enforcement action for firms that fall short.
